toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect dump on crash

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect dump on crash

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9 MSTest project. The user wants to run the tests and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process.

For .NET, crash dumps can be collected by configuring environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory)
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump

Alternatively, `dotnet test` with `--blame-crash` flag from the Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk will collect crash dumps. This is the more standard/recommended approach for test scenarios.


I'll go with `dotnet test --blame-crash` since it's the idiomatic way to handle crash dumps in .NET testing.

data.content:
All 3 tests passed, no crashes. The `--blame-crash` flag was active, so if any test had crashed the host process, a dump file would have been collected automatically.

**Command used:**
```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

This configures the test platform to monitor for host crashes and write a crash dump (via `procdump` / `createdump`) if the test host process dies unexpectedly. Since all tests passed cleanly, no dump was generated.
